close_reason: Cache amplification on transient failure is correct: retry logic belongs at the caller, not in resolve_project_id
description: src/lib.rs:286-294 wraps the entire (auth + resolve_project + resolve_secret) chain in a single get_or_try_init. If auth succeeds and resolve_project_id succeeds but resolve_secret_id fails (e.g. a transient 5xx on list_by_project), the OnceCell stays uninitialized — so the next get() call re-authenticates AND re-lists projects, even though both already succeeded. For a long-lived backend that hits a flaky network, this amplifies failed-call cost from 'one re-list' to 'auth + re-list-projects + re-list-secrets' on every retry. Fix: split the cache into nested OnceCells or a small state machine — Authed(client, org_id) → ProjectResolved(client, org_id, project_id) → SecretResolved(client, org_id, project_id, secret_id). Each layer caches independently. This is a real efficiency win for transient-failure scenarios but adds complexity; defer until evidence of actual problem in the wild.
title: Cache amplification: list_projects re-runs on every transient failure
